data.content: Now let me check the layout components to understand the dashboard and logo-only layouts:

Here's a complete breakdown of how routing is organized in this React admin app:

---

## Routing Architecture Overview

The app uses **React Router v6** (`react-router-dom`) with `<BrowserRouter>` wrapping the entire application (set up in `src/main.tsx`). All route definitions live in a single file: **`src/routes.tsx`**.

---

## Two Top-Level Layout Branches

The `<Routes>` tree is split into exactly **two top-level `<Route>` groups**, each with its own layout wrapper:

### 1. Dashboard Layout (`/dashboard/*`)

```tsx
<Route path="/dashboard" element={<DashboardLayout />}>
    <Route path=""   element={<Navigate to="/dashboard/app" replace />} />
    <Route path="app"       element={<DashboardApp />} />
    <Route path="user"      element={<User />} />
    <Route path="products"  element={<Products />} />
    <Route path="blog"      element={<Blog />} />
</Route>
```

- **Layout:** `DashboardLayout` (`src/layouts/dashboard/index.tsx`) renders a classic admin shell — a collapsible **sidebar** (`DashboardSidebar`) + a **top navbar** (`DashboardNavbar`) + a content area via `<Outlet />`.
- **Default redirect:** Hitting exactly `/dashboard` (with no sub-path) immediately `<Navigate>`s to `/dashboard/app` with `replace`, so the user lands on the main dashboard overview.
- **Sidebar navigation items** (defined in `SidebarConfig.tsx`) link to: `/dashboard/app`, `/dashboard/user`, `/dashboard/products`, `/dashboard/blog`, plus auth pages (`/login`, `/register`) and the 404 page (`/404`).

### 2. Logo-Only Layout (`/` and its children)

```tsx
<Route path="/" element={<LogoOnlyLayout />}>
    <Route path="login"    element={<Login />} />
    <Route path="register" element={<Register />} />
    <Route path="404"      element={<NotFound />} />
    <Route path=""         element={<Navigate to="/dashboard" />} />
    <Route path="*"        element={<Navigate to="/404" />} />
</Route>
```

- **Layout:** `LogoOnlyLayout` (`src/layouts/LogoOnlyLayout.tsx`) renders only the **Logo** in the top-left corner (clickable, links back to `/`) and an `<Outlet />` for the child route content. No sidebar, no navbar.

---

## Auth-Related Pages

| Route | Component | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `/login` | `Login` | Renders inside `LogoOnlyLayout`. Uses its own inner `AuthLayout` component to show a "Don't have an account? Get started" link to `/register`. The login page itself has a two-column split (illustration on the left, login form on the right). |
| `/register` | `Register` | Same pattern — renders inside `LogoOnlyLayout`, similarly uses `AuthLayout` for header content, links back to `/login`. |

> **Note:** `AuthLayout` (`src/layouts/AuthLayout.tsx`) is **not** a route-level layout — it's a presentation component imported directly by `Login` and `Register` to render the header with logo + a right-side text slot (e.g., "Don't have an account?"). It does **not** wrap `<Outlet />`.

---

## Redirects

There are **three redirects** in the app:

| Source Path | Target | Behavior |
|---|---|---|
| `/dashboard` (exact) | `/dashboard/app` | `replace` — replaces the history entry so the user can't back-navigate to the bare `/dashboard` path. |
| `/` (exact) | `/dashboard` | Standard redirect (no `replace`) — visiting the root URL sends the user to the dashboard. |
| `/*` (any unmatched path) | `/404` | Catch-all redirect to the 404 page. |

---

## Fallback 404 Behavior

The 404 handling works as follows:

1. **Catch-all `<Route path="*">`** inside the LogoOnlyLayout group redirects any unmatched URL to `/404`.
2. **`/404`** renders the `Page404` component (`src/pages/Page404.tsx`) within the LogoOnlyLayout — so it shows just the logo header.
3. The `Page404` component displays:
   - An animated "Sorry, page not found!" heading (using Framer Motion's `varBounceIn`).
   - A descriptive message suggesting the user check the URL.
   - An SVG illustration.
   - A **"Go to Home" button** that links back to `/` (which then redirects to `/dashboard`).

---

## Data Flow Summary

```
/
├── /login        → LogoOnlyLayout → Login (with AuthLayout header)
├── /register     → LogoOnlyLayout → Register (with AuthLayout header)
├── /404          → LogoOnlyLayout → Page404
├── (exact "")    → Navigate → /dashboard
├── *             → Navigate → /404
│
/dashboard
├── (exact "")    → Navigate → /dashboard/app (replace)
├── /app          → DashboardLayout → DashboardApp
├── /user         → DashboardLayout → User
├── /products     → DashboardLayout → Products
└── /blog         → DashboardLayout → Blog
```

---

## Key Design Observations

- **No route guards or auth protection:** There are no private route wrappers or authentication checks on the dashboard routes. All dashboard pages are publicly accessible.
- **No nested route protection:** The `AuthLayout` is purely cosmetic — it doesn't enforce any auth logic.
- **Single-file routing:** All route definitions are centralized in `src/routes.tsx` rather than being split across feature modules.
- **Layout nesting uses React Router v6's `<Outlet />` pattern:** Both `DashboardLayout` and `LogoOnlyLayout` render `<Outlet />` to display their child routes.
- **The 404 page lives under the root (`/`) layout**, not under `/dashboard`, so unmatched dashboard sub-routes (e.g., `/dashboard/nonexistent`) will still redirect to `/404` via the catch-all `*` route — but notably, this catch-all is at the *root* layout level, meaning any path not matched by the `/dashboard/*` group falls through to the `/*` handler.
